titleStmt.title: Letter from S. M. O'Meara to Anna O'Rahilly
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: S. M. O'Meara

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from S. M. O'Meara to Anna O'Rahilly, the sister of Michael Joseph O'Rahilly (1875-1916), an Irish volunteer who was killed on Moore Street during the 1916 Easter Rising while trying to find a way out of the General Post Office. The letter concerns money which the author states Anna will soon receive. The letter goes on to discuss the distribution of the money through a maintenance committee.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Shelbourne Hotel
Dublin Tel 4291 3 lines
My dear Miss O’Rahilly You will soon receive
a few thousand pounds from Limerick
Send receipt in duplicate to me
to Browns Hotel Dover
St London. The amount would
be considerably larger
had you acted in regard
to distribution of
other sums in a less
parochial spirit. I am now retaining
sufficient for Limerick
needs for about three
months. I regret that
this course has been forced
upon me as no fund can really be
fairly distributed except
through a national
committee. I expect in
a few months to receive other sums which I hope
to be able to hand over
to you as it is not my
intention to divert your
revenues unless you force
me to do so by again
refusing to advance a penny to relieve distress
in such places as Limerick whilst you
are distributing £750 per
week in Dublin. Mise le meas S. M. Ó Meára
